sites_403_404: https://www.tucsonweekly.com/ contact pages (no individual emails published)

high_yield_publishers: Public-radio newsrooms (KJZZ) publish complete staff directories with per-person contact via Cloudflare-encoded mailto. Decoded data-cfemail attributes give clean named-contact pairs.
Independent investigative nonprofits (AZCIR) publish full staff list with role-tagged emails on /about/.

Government masthead pages (azleg.gov MemberRoster) publish all legislators' email column directly in the roster table — explicit and verifiable, not pattern-derived because the column header itself reads 'Email @azleg.gov' and the prefix is literal.
low_yield_or_blocked: Cloudflare bot challenge blocks legacy Gannett/Lee/Voice Media outlets (azcentral, tucson.com, tucsonsentinel, phoenixnewtimes, tucsonweekly) from any verifiable extraction. Their staff pages exist but require a real browser session to view.
Local TV station bio pages (ABC15, 12News, AZ Family) do not publish reporter emails — only generic newsroom intake addresses.

Government regulator pages (AZ Board of Pharmacy, DEA Phoenix, AZ AG Consumer Protection) do not publish individual staff emails — they route press through generic webforms or aginfo@azag.gov-style aliases. Skipped per generic-intake rule.
Academic pharmacy schools (UA Coit, Midwestern, ASU) have faculty pages behind directory search rather than scrapeable email-listed mastheads.

Sonoran Prevention Works lists individual contact-form-routing emails (jpatrick@, hcoles@, jwitowski@, tcarrasco@) in the form action attribute, but their /our-team/ roster shows different staff names (Greenwood as CEO, Fox as Comms Manager, etc.). The form-action emails couldn't be confidently mapped to specific named people on the same page, so SPW staff omitted to avoid the pattern-derived violation.

person_first_language_reminder: All outreach copy to these contacts must use 'people who experience opioid use disorder,' 'families who lost a loved one,' 'people in recovery' — never 'addicts' or 'victims.'
next_harvest_recommendations: AZ Mirror staff emails are publicly listed but blocked by Cloudflare for headless fetch. Use a real browser session (Chrome MCP) to capture: Caitlin Sievers (health/accountability), Gloria Rebecca Gomez (politics/policy), Jerod MacDonald-Evoy (investigative), Jim Small (editor).
Tucson Sentinel masthead similarly blocked. Worth a Chrome MCP pass for Dylan Smith, Paul Ingram, Bennito L. Kelty.
AZCentral staff pages locked behind Cloudflare + login. Best path is bylines on past AZ opioid stories captured via SimilarWeb or article-page scraping rather than masthead.
Sonoran Prevention Works: phone the press line documented at spwaz.org/contact/ to request a named press contact, then enrich and add.
